pi_items.source: Code analysis: export_result.sh statically defines REPORT_PATH as this value.
Code analysis: export_result.sh statically defines SCREENSHOT_PATH as this value.
Web search: 0.52 is widely recognized as the standard medical ellipsoid volume formula multiplier. Code analysis: verifier.py uses 0.52 to evaluate expected volume.
Code analysis: verifier.py directly uses 5.0 to check if reported dimensions are plausible.
Code analysis: verifier.py compares percent difference of reported volume against a 5% (0.05) tolerance.

privileged_info_summary: The task dynamically generates a synthetic 2D CT DICOM image with a single bright elliptical mass (120x80 mm). Based on code analysis, the grading expects a report at '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/volume_report.json' and a screenshot at '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/volume_measurements.png'. The verifier mathematically expects the reported volume to equal the product of the three provided dimensions (each must be > 5.0 mm) multiplied by 0.52 within a 5% tolerance. Medical literature independently confirms 0.52 as the standard ellipsoid formula multiplier. Since the image is 2D, the user must hypothesize a third dimension, but the verifier only evaluates mathematical consistency, not the lengths' accuracy against the image.
